mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered cases or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion periods, with a hit rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strengthening consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across all market conditions over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 230.0, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases or shows no statistical edge over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic strength, which supports equity earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during growth phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns during expansion phases, with a directional accuracy below 50 percent.
